name: Glide Ratio Calculator
description: Compute glide ratio using lift and drag forces.
formulaDescription: Glide ratio is the distance traveled horizontally for each unit of descent and is approximated by L/D.

example.explanation: A lift of 4500 N with 130 N of drag yields a glide ratio of about 34.6.

faqs.question: What is a good glide ratio?
faqs.answer: Glide ratios above 20 are considered good for sailplanes, while typical light aircraft have lower ratios.
